class.name: AutoworkTest
class.description: Core class for the Autowork framework: AutoworkTest. Enables native testing, assertions, and mock implementations within Blazium Engine.

method.name: _after_all
method.description: Utility method: after all.

method.name: _after_each
method.description: Utility method: after each.

method.name: _before_all
method.description: Utility method: before all.

method.name: _before_each
method.description: Utility method: before each.

method.name: add_child_autofree
method.description: Adds a child node to the test sandbox and schedules it for automatic deletion at the end of the test.

method.name: add_child_autoqfree
method.description: Adds a child node to the test sandbox and schedules it for automatic deletion at the end of the test. [codeblock] var ret_child = add_child_autoqfree(new_child) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_accessors
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_accessors(node, "position", Vector2(100, 100), Vector2(200, 200)) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_almost_eq
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is equal to [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_almost_eq(1.0001, 1.0, 0.01, "Close enough float") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_almost_ne
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is not equal to [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_almost_ne(1.5, 1.0, 0.01, "Not close float") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_between
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is between [param expect_low] and [param expect_high]. [codeblock] assert_between(5, 1, 10, "5 is between 1 and 10") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_call_count
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_call_count(self, "get_parent", 0, []) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_called
method.description: Asserts that a method was called on a stubbed or spied object. [codeblock] assert_called(obj, "call_me_maybe", [1, 2]) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_called_count
method.description: Asserts that a method was called on a stubbed or spied object.

method.name: assert_connected
method.description: Asserts that [param source] and [param target] are connected via [param signal]. [codeblock] assert_connected(base, self, "child_entered_tree", "pass_test") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_dir_does_not_exist
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_dir_exists
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_dir_exists("res://") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_does_not_have
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_does_not_have({"a": 1, "b": 2}, "c", "Dict does not have key c") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_eq
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is equal to [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_eq(2 + 2, 4, "Inner group two math works") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_eq_deep
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is equal to [param expected].

method.name: assert_eq_shallow
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is equal to [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_eq_shallow(1, 1, "Testing basic assert_eq_shallow alias") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_exports
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_extends
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_false
method.description: Asserts that [param condition] is false. [codeblock] assert_false(false, "Inner group one: false is false") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_file_does_not_exist
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_file_does_not_exist("res://this_does_not_exist.txt") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_file_empty
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_file_exists
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_file_not_empty
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_freed
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_ge
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_gt
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_gt(5, 3) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_gte
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_gte(gut.get_test_count(), 0, "gut.get_test_count is accessible") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_has
method.description: Asserts that the object or collection has the specified element/property/method. [codeblock] assert_has([1, 2, 3], 2, "Array has 2") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_has_method
method.description: Asserts that the object or collection has the specified element/property/method. [codeblock] assert_has_method(self, "test_expanded_asserts_pass") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_has_signal
method.description: Asserts that the object or collection has the specified element/property/method. [codeblock] assert_has_signal(node, "draw", "Node2D has 'draw' signal") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_is
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_le
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_lt
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_lt(2, 10) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_lte
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_lte(gut.get_fail_count(), 100, "gut.get_fail_count is accessible") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_ne
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is not equal to [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_ne("hello", "world") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_ne_deep
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is not equal to [param expected].

method.name: assert_ne_shallow
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is not equal to [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_ne_shallow(1, 2, "Testing basic assert_ne_shallow alias") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_no_new_orphans
method.description: Asserts that no new orphaned instances were created since the test began.

method.name: assert_not_between
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is between [param expect_low] and [param expect_high]. [codeblock] assert_not_between(15, 1, 10, "15 is not between 1 and 10") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_not_called
method.description: Asserts that a method was called on a stubbed or spied object. [codeblock] assert_not_called(obj, "get_name") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_not_connected
method.description: Asserts that [param source] and [param target] are NOT connected via [param signal]. [codeblock] assert_not_connected(base, self, "child_exiting_tree", "fail_test") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_not_freed
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_not_freed(temp) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_not_null
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_not_null(leaked, "Node created but not freed") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_not_same
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_not_typeof
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_null
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_property
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_property(node, "position", Vector2(0, 0), Vector2(100, 100)) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_property_with_backing_variable
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_readonly_property
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_same
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_same(ret_child, new_child, "Returned child is the same instance") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_set_property
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_setget
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed.

method.name: assert_signal_emit_count
method.description: Asserts that a signal was emitted by the target object. [codeblock] assert_signal_emit_count(node, "draw", 2, "Signal 'draw' was emitted twice") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_signal_emitted
method.description: Asserts that a signal was emitted by the target object. [codeblock] assert_signal_emitted(self, "my_custom_signal", "Verified custom signal") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_signal_emitted_with_parameters
method.description: Asserts that a signal was emitted by the target object. [codeblock] assert_signal_emitted_with_parameters(node, "child_entered_tree", [node], 0, "Signal parameters matched") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_signal_not_emitted
method.description: Asserts that a signal was emitted by the target object. [codeblock] assert_signal_not_emitted(self, "another_signal", "Verified not emitted") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_string_contains
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_string_contains("hello world", "world") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_string_ends_with
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_string_ends_with("hello world", "orld") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_string_starts_with
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_string_starts_with("hello world", "hell") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_true
method.description: Asserts that [param condition] is true. [codeblock] assert_true(true, "Inner group one: true is true") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_typeof
method.description: Asserts a specific condition during tests. If the condition fails, the test is marked as failed. [codeblock] assert_typeof(5, TYPE_INT) [/codeblock]

method.name: autofree
method.description: Utility method: autofree.

method.name: autoqfree
method.description: Utility method: autoqfree.

method.name: clear_parameters
method.description: Utility method: clear parameters.

method.name: clear_text
method.description: Utility method: clear text.

method.name: compare_deep
method.description: Utility method: compare deep.

method.name: compare_shallow
method.description: Utility method: compare shallow.

method.name: create_double
method.description: Utility method: create double.

method.name: did_wait_timeout
method.description: Utility method: did wait timeout.

method.name: directory_delete_files
method.description: Utility method: directory delete files.

method.name: double_inner
method.description: Creates a test double (mock/stub) of a specific resource, scene, or script. [codeblock] # double_inner("res://fake.gd", "InnerClass") [/codeblock]

method.name: double_resource
method.description: Creates a test double (mock/stub) of a specific resource, scene, or script.

method.name: double_scene
method.description: Creates a test double (mock/stub) of a specific resource, scene, or script.

method.name: double_script
method.description: Creates a test double (mock/stub) of a specific resource, scene, or script.

method.name: double_singleton
method.description: Creates a test double (mock/stub) of a specific resource, scene, or script. [codeblock] double_singleton("FakeSingletonName") [/codeblock]

method.name: fail_test
method.description: Utility method: fail test.

method.name: file_delete
method.description: Utility method: file delete.

method.name: file_touch
method.description: Utility method: file touch.

method.name: get_assert_count
method.description: Utility method: get assert count. [codeblock] assert_true(get_assert_count() > 0, "Assert count getter binds") [/codeblock]

method.name: get_call_count
method.description: Utility method: get call count.

method.name: get_call_parameters
method.description: Utility method: get call parameters.

method.name: get_current_parameter_index
method.description: Utility method: get current parameter index.

method.name: get_fail_count
method.description: Utility method: get fail count. [codeblock] assert_eq(get_fail_count(), 0, "There should be 0 failure tests from older intentional fail tests") [/codeblock]

method.name: get_file_as_text
method.description: Utility method: get file as text.

method.name: get_logger
method.description: Utility method: get logger. [codeblock] assert_not_null(gut.get_logger(), "gut.logger is accessible natively") [/codeblock]

method.name: get_parameter_count
method.description: Utility method: get parameter count.

method.name: get_pass_count
method.description: Utility method: get pass count. [codeblock] assert_true(get_pass_count() > 0, "Pass count getter binds") [/codeblock]

method.name: get_pending_count
method.description: Utility method: get pending count. [codeblock] assert_eq(get_pending_count(), 2, "There should be 0 pending test from older hooks") [/codeblock]

method.name: get_signal_emit_count
method.description: Utility method: get signal emit count.

method.name: get_signal_parameters
method.description: Utility method: get signal parameters.

method.name: get_summary
method.description: Utility method: get summary. [codeblock] var summary_dict = get_summary() [/codeblock]

method.name: get_summary_text
method.description: Utility method: get summary text. [codeblock] var summary_txt = get_summary_text() [/codeblock]

method.name: get_test_count
method.description: Returns the current count of get test count. [codeblock] assert_gte(gut.get_test_count(), 0, "gut.get_test_count is accessible") [/codeblock]

method.name: has_parameters
method.description: Utility method: has parameters.

method.name: ignore_method_when_doubling
method.description: Utility method: ignore method when doubling.

method.name: is_file_empty
method.description: Utility method: is file empty.

method.name: maximize
method.description: Utility method: maximize.

method.name: p
method.description: Prints a message to the Autowork logger console natively.

method.name: partial_double
method.description: Utility method: partial double.

method.name: partial_double_inner
method.description: Utility method: partial double inner.

method.name: partial_double_singleton
method.description: Utility method: partial double singleton.

method.name: pass_test
method.description: Utility method: pass test. [codeblock] pass_test("Forced pass") [/codeblock]

method.name: pause_before_teardown
method.description: Utility method: pause before teardown.

method.name: pending
method.description: Utility method: pending. [codeblock] # pending("Disabled missing key dictionary diff logging test natively as C++ deep arrays print structurally differently") [/codeblock]

method.name: register_inner_classes
method.description: Utility method: register inner classes.

method.name: replace_node
method.description: Utility method: replace node.

method.name: set_current_parameter_index
method.description: Utility method: set current parameter index.

method.name: set_doubler
method.description: Utility method: set doubler.

method.name: set_logger
method.description: Utility method: set logger.

method.name: set_spy
method.description: Utility method: set spy.

method.name: set_stubber
method.description: Utility method: set stubber.

method.name: show_orphans
method.description: Utility method: show orphans.

method.name: simulate
method.description: Utility method: simulate.

method.name: skip_if_godot_version_lt
method.description: Utility method: skip if godot version lt.

method.name: skip_if_godot_version_ne
method.description: Utility method: skip if godot version ne.

method.name: spy
method.description: Utility method: spy.

method.name: stub
method.description: Utility method: stub.

method.name: use_parameters
method.description: Utility method: use parameters.

method.name: wait_for_signal
method.description: Suspends the coroutine execution until a specific amount of time or frames have elapsed.

method.name: wait_frames
method.description: Suspends the coroutine execution until a specific amount of time or frames have elapsed. [codeblock] # await wait_frames(2, "Wait frames completed") [/codeblock]

method.name: wait_idle_frames
method.description: Suspends the coroutine execution until a specific amount of time or frames have elapsed. [codeblock] # await wait_idle_frames(1, "Testing idle frames wrapper") [/codeblock]

method.name: wait_physics_frames
method.description: Suspends the coroutine execution until a specific amount of time or frames have elapsed. [codeblock] # await wait_physics_frames(2, "Wait physics frames completed") [/codeblock]

method.name: wait_process_frames
method.description: Suspends the coroutine execution until a specific amount of time or frames have elapsed.

method.name: wait_seconds
method.description: Suspends the coroutine execution until a specific amount of time or frames have elapsed. [codeblock] # await wait_seconds(0.1, "Wait 0.1s completed") [/codeblock]

method.name: wait_until
method.description: Suspends the coroutine execution until a specific amount of time or frames have elapsed.

method.name: wait_while
method.description: Suspends the coroutine execution until a specific amount of time or frames have elapsed.

method.name: watch_signal
method.description: Utility method: watch signal.

method.name: watch_signals
method.description: Utility method: watch signals.

method.name: yield_for
method.description: Utility method: yield for.

method.name: yield_frames
method.description: Utility method: yield frames.

method.name: yield_to
method.description: Utility method: yield to.
